answer:
Let the width be \( w \) meters. Then the length is \( w + 14 \) meters.  
The perimeter is \( 2(w + (w + 14)) = 96 \), so \( 2(2w + 14) = 96 \) → \( 4w + 28 = 96 \) → \( 4w = 68 \) → \( w = 17 \).  
Thus the width is 17 meters and the length is \( 17 + 14 = 31 \) meters.

\boxed{17 \text{ m} \times 31 \text{ m}}
